zh.lifeSummary: Pietro di Celano是Comte Berardo di Albe的兒子，於大約1159年出生。他和Miss di Palearia至少有1個兒子。他去世於從1212年8月8日到1212年10月7日。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Pietro di Celano是Comte Berardo di Albe的儿子，于大约1159年出生。他和Miss di Palearia至少有1个儿子。他去世于从1212年8月8日到1212年10月7日。

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Celano
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Fiore, Guido, Osvaldo, Tullio, Vito, Antonio, Bernardo, Geno, Giuseppe, Leonardo, Marino, Mario, Nino, Orazio.
Italian: habitational name from a place called Celano, in L'Aquila province.

variants.name: Celani
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Domenic, Dario, Gabriele, Matteo, Natale, Salvatore, Vincenzo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Celano  .

nameMeanings.name: Pietro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Piero
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Pietro di Celano was born about 1159 as the son of Comte Berardo di Albe. He had at least 1 son with Miss di Palearia. He died from 8 August 1212 to 7 October 1212.

fr.lifeSummary: Pietro di Celano est né environ 1159, fils de Comte Berardo di Albe. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Miss di Palearia. Il est décédé du 8 août 1212 au 7 octobre 1212.

de.lifeSummary: Pietro di Celano wurde um 1159, als Sohn von Comte Berardo di Albe geboren. Er hatte mit Miss di Palearia mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb von 8. August 1212 bis 7. Oktober 1212.

it.lifeSummary: Pietro di Celano è nato il circa 1159 come figlio di Comte Berardo di Albe. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Miss di Palearia. Morì da 8 agosto 1212 a 7 ottobre 1212.
